segments.p2-7-g370: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and' left hanging here as an abrupt trailing conjunction, showing the sentence breaks off (ellipsis) and continues the thought in the next sentence.
segments.p2-7-g373: Вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly.' Я is the subject; остановился is the past-tense reflexive verb 'stopped/paused.' Word order is typical: adverb — subject — verb.
segments.p2-7-g374: Приподнялся is past tense 'lifted/rose slightly' (perfective). В испуге is a prepositional phrase 'in alarm/in a fright' (в + noun in prepositional), and и coordinates to the following participial clause.
segments.p2-7-g375: Наклонив is a verbal adverb/gerund (деепричастие) 'having tilted' or 'tilting' and links this accompanying action to the main verb; боязливо is an adverb 'timidly/fearfully'; голову is the direct object in accusative ('head') — the normal collocation наклонить голову 'to tilt the head.'
segments.p2-7-g376: С + instrumental (с бьющимся сердцем) means 'with a beating heart' — бьющимся is a present participle in instrumental agreeing with сердцем. Начал is past tense 'began' and is followed by the imperfective infinitive прислушиваться, which expresses beginning a durative action: 'began to listen (carefully).'
segments.p2-7-g377: Было is an impersonal past-tense neuter form used existentially ('there was'). от чего is a genitive prepositional phrase expressing cause or 'for which reason' (literally 'from what'), and и here is a particle meaning 'even/also' adding emphasis. Смутиться is an infinitive meaning 'to become embarrassed' and completes the impersonal construction (Было от чего смутиться = 'there was reason to be embarrassed').
segments.p2-7-g238: 'И что,' is a rhetorical opener: 'и' is the coordinating 'and' attaching this clause to the preceding thought, and 'что' here functions as an exclamatory/introducing particle yielding the sense 'what if...'.
segments.p2-7-g239: 'если в ту самую минуту' is a conditional temporal phrase: 'если' = 'if', 'в' marks the time ('in/during'), 'ту самую минуту' = 'that very minute' with 'ту' + emphatic 'самую' selecting the exact moment.
segments.p2-7-g240: 'когда она колотила этой рыбой о грязные ступени' is a past continuous description: 'когда' = 'when', 'она' = 'she', 'колотила' is past imperfect ('was beating/pounding'), 'этой рыбой' is instrumental ('with that fish'), and 'о грязные ступени' uses 'о' in the sense 'against' — 'beat against the dirty steps.'
segments.p2-7-g241: 'пьяная да растрепанная' is a two-adjective description: 'пьяная' = 'drunk'; 'да' here is a colloquial/archaic conjunction meaning 'and'; 'растрепанная' = 'tousled/dishevelled.'
segments.p2-7-g242: This short 'что,' repeats the exclamatory/introductory particle ('what if') to continue the hypothetical chain begun earlier; it is not the 'that' of reported speech here.
segments.p2-7-g243: 'если в ту минуту ей припомнились все ее прежние' — another conditional: 'если' = 'if', 'в ту минуту' = 'at that moment'; 'ей припомнились' is a reflexive past-verb meaning 'came to her mind' or 'she recalled' (the verb agrees with the recalled items, hence plural form); 'все её прежние' = 'all her former ones' (ellipsis for 'years').
segments.p2-7-g244: 'чистые годы в отцовском доме' is a noun phrase: 'чистые' = 'pure' or 'innocent' (qualifying childhood/years), 'годы' = 'years', and 'в отцовском доме' = 'in the father's house' (locative place of those years).
segments.p2-7-g245: 'когда ещё она в школу ходила' is a past habitual clause: 'когда' = 'when/once', 'ещё' = 'still/then', 'она' = 'she', 'в школу ходила' = 'used to go to school' (ходила is past imperfective, habitual 'went/attended').
segments.p2-7-g246: 'а соседский сын её на дороге подстерегал' continues the past narrative: 'а' = contrasting 'and/but', 'соседский сын' = 'the neighbour's son', 'её' (accusative) = 'her', 'на дороге' = 'on the road', 'подстерегал' = past imperfective 'lay in wait / watched for' (habitual action in the past).
segments.p2-7-g247: 'уверял' is a past imperfective verb 'he assured/kept assuring' and introduces reported speech that follows; it's third person singular masculine in the past.
segments.p2-7-g248: The clause 'что всю жизнь ее любить будет' is reported content: 'что' = 'that'; 'всю жизнь' = 'all his life'; 'ее' = 'her' (accusative); 'любить будет' is the analytic future ('will love' — infinitive + auxiliary 'будет'), common in spoken Russian.
segments.p2-7-g249: 'что судьбу свою ей положит' continues the reported promises: 'что' = 'that'; 'судьбу свою' = 'his own fate' (accusative phrase meaning 'his future/life'); 'ей' = dative 'to her'; 'положит' = future 'will put/lay' — here idiomatic: 'will place (his) fate with her' = 'will give his life/future to her.'
segments.p2-7-g250: 'и когда они вместе положили любить друг друга навеки и обвенчаться' — 'и' = 'and'; 'когда' = 'when/once'; 'они вместе положили' uses 'положили' in a colloquial/figurative sense 'decided/pledged' (here recorded with the sense 'agree/resolve to'); it governs the following infinitives 'любить' and 'обвенчаться' = 'to love' and 'to be married in church'; 'друг друга' is the reciprocal structure 'each other'; 'навеки' = 'forever.'
segments.p2-7-g251: 'только что вырастут большие!' is an exclamatory rejoinder: 'только' = 'just/wait and see', 'что' is an exclamatory particle here, and 'вырастут большие' = future 'they will grow up (big/old)' — colloquial promise/resolution 'just you wait till they're grown.'
segments.p2-7-g252: 'Нет,' is a blunt negative interjection 'No' used to contradict or start a sharp reply.
segments.p2-7-g253: 'Лиза,' is direct address (vocative) — the addressee's name.
segments.p2-7-g254: 'счастье,' here is used as an interjectional noun 'happiness' (ironically invoked), repeated in the next segment for emphasis.
segments.p2-7-g255: 'счастье тебе,' is a dative expression 'happiness to you' (literally 'happiness to you'), with 'тебе' marking the recipient; the phrase is ironic/wishful in tone.
segments.p2-7-g256: 'если где-нибудь там' begins a conditional 'if somewhere there': 'если' = 'if', 'где' = 'where', 'нибудь' = indefinite particle ('any/ever' softening), 'там' = 'there' (remote place).
segments.p2-7-g257: 'в углу' = 'in a corner': 'в' marks location and 'углу' is the locative/prepositional form meaning 'in the corner' (a secluded spot).
segments.p2-7-g258: 'в подвале' = 'in the cellar/basement': same locative construction; it specifies an even lower, more wretched place.
segments.p2-7-g259: 'как давешняя' is a comparative/recall phrase: 'как' = 'like/as', 'давешняя' = 'the one from the other day' or 'recent (one)' — an elliptical reference to some earlier woman/incident.
segments.p2-7-g260: 'в чахотке поскорее помрёшь' = 'you'll die sooner of consumption': 'в чахотке' = 'of consumption' (prepositional phrase naming the disease), 'поскорее' = 'sooner/sooner rather' (colloquial adverb), 'помрёшь' = future second-person singular 'you will die' (colloquial prefix-po 'po-' plus imperfective stem used to mean 'to die' here).
segments.p2-7-g261: 'В больницу,' is an accusative phrase with 'в' indicating motion toward a place: 'to the hospital' (destination).
segments.p2-7-g262: 'говоришь ты?' is a colloquial question tag: 'говоришь' = 'you say' (present tense, used rhetorically), 'ты' = 'you' (emphatic subject). Together it challenges or repeats the addressee's suggestion.
segments.p2-7-g263: 'Хорошо - свезут,' is colloquial: 'Хорошо' = 'all right/okay'; the dash marks a quick result; 'свезут' is future 3rd-person plural of 'свезти' meaning 'they will bring/transport (her)', often by wagon/vehicle.
segments.p2-7-g264: 'а если ты еще хозяйке нужна?' is a counter-question: 'а' = 'but/and', 'если' = 'if', 'ты' = 'you', 'еще' = 'still/yet', 'хозяйке' = dative 'to the landlady/householder', 'нужна' = predicative adjective 'needed' (here 'are you still needed by the landlady?'). Note the dative agent + predicate construction common in Russian ('ты нужна хозяйке').
segments.p2-7-g400: 'Но' is a coordinating conjunction meaning 'but' used to contrast the previous clause; 'теперь' is an adverb 'now' indicating the moment just described. The comma marks a pause before the adverbial participle phrase that follows.
segments.p2-7-g401: 'достигнув' is a деепричастие (adverbial participle) formed from the perfective verb 'достигнуть' and means 'having attained/achieved'; it qualifies the subject's state at that moment and takes a genitive object 'эффекта'. This participial phrase gives the cause/preceding action to the main clause.
segments.p2-7-g402: 'я' is the nominative subject 'I'; 'вдруг' is an adverb 'suddenly' modifying the verb; 'струсил' is past tense, masculine singular of the colloquial verb 'струсить' meaning 'to chicken out / be cowardly' (perfective past). Together they form the main clause: 'I suddenly chickened out.'
segments.p2-7-g403: 'Нет' is an exclamatory negation here, functioning like 'no' to reject or interrupt; punctuation marks the emphatic exclamation.
segments.p2-7-g404: 'никогда' is an adverb meaning 'never' and here begins an emphatic repetition ('никогда, никогда') used for strong negation and emotional emphasis.
segments.p2-7-g405: The phrase 'никогда ещё' is an adverbial combination meaning 'never yet / never before'; 'я не был свидетелем' is a past negative construction ('I have not been a witness')—Russian uses double negation for emphasis. 'такого отчаяния' is a genitive noun phrase 'of such despair', specifying the kind of thing he has never witnessed.
segments.p2-7-g406: 'Она' is the feminine third-person subject 'she'; 'лежала' is the past tense, imperfective verb 'lay' (describing a sustained posture); 'ничком' is an adverb meaning 'face down' or 'prone' and follows the verb to describe the position.
segments.p2-7-g407: 'крепко' is an adverb 'firmly'; 'уткнув' and 'обхватив' are two adverbial participles (деепричастия, both perfective) modifying 'лежала' and describing simultaneous actions: 'уткнув (лицо в подушку)' = 'having pressed/buried her face into the pillow' (with 'в' + accusative indicating 'into'), and 'обхватив её обеими руками' = 'having clasped it with both hands' (обеими руками in the instrumental marks the means). The participles express manner of lying.
segments.p2-7-g408: 'Ей' is a dative pronoun marking the experiencer in an impersonal construction; 'разрывало' is an impersonal past-tense form (neuter singular) of 'разрывать/разрывáть' used to express that something was happening to her (emotionally wrenching); 'грудь' is the accusative object 'chest'. The construction means 'her chest was being torn' or figuratively 'she felt as if her chest were being torn.'
segments.p2-7-g271: An imperative with the negative particle. Не precedes the reflexive verb беспокоиться in the familiar singular imperative беспокойся: 'don't worry' or 'do not be anxious.'
segments.p2-7-g272: A short parenthetical statement: это 'this/that/it' points to the point under discussion and так 'so' here means 'that's how/that is' — i.e. 'that's so' / 'that's true.'
segments.p2-7-g273: Accusative singular душу (from душа) is the direct object of продала that follows; here it is used literally/figuratively as 'soul' (sold her soul).
segments.p2-7-g274: Parenthetical discourse particle: значит here is a filler/inferential marker meaning 'so/you see/then' and does not introduce a new clause tense-wise.
segments.p2-7-g275: Past tense, feminine singular of продать: продала = 'she sold' (subject is omitted but understood from context). It carries the literal/figurative meaning 'sold' (as in 'sold her soul').
segments.p2-7-g276: А к тому же is a fixed phrase meaning 'and besides / on top of that.' Деньги должна uses деньги (direct object) with the short form predicate должна (feminine) meaning 'owes money' or 'is owing money' — the feminine agrees with the omitted subject 'she.'
segments.p2-7-g277: Another значит used inferentially ('so/therefore/then'); и here functions as an intensifier 'even/also' ('even to squeak'); пикнуть is a colloquial perfective infinitive 'to let out a peep/squeak'; не смеешь is present 2nd-person singular of сметь meaning 'you do not dare' — together 'you can't even so much as squeak.'
segments.p2-7-g278: А is a coordinating particle ('and/but'). Умирать будешь places the infinitive умирать with the auxiliary будешь (2nd-person singular future of быть) to form an imperfective future sense: 'you will be dying' / colloquially 'you'll die' in the sense of an ongoing or inevitable dying.
segments.p2-7-g279: Все ('everyone/they all') is the subject; тебя is the accusative object 'you'; бросят is 3rd-person plural future of бросить and here means 'they will abandon/leave (you).' Word order is subject–object–verb (typical Russian) with the future tense expressed by a perfective verb in future form.
segments.p2-7-g280: Again все as subject 'everyone'; отвернутся is 3rd-person plural future of the reflexive verb отвернуться 'to turn away' (turn one's back). The reflexive suffix -ся marks the intransitive action 'turn away.'
segments.p2-7-g281: Потому is a short connective adverb meaning 'for that reason' and here introduces the explanation clause that follows (usually completed by что).
segments.p2-7-g282: A subordinate question clause: что is the interrogative 'what'; с + genitive (с тебя) = 'from you'; тогда 'then/in that case'; взять (infinitive) 'to take' — together forming the rhetorical question 'what can (they) take from you then?'
segments.p2-7-g283: Еще here means 'yet/still' or 'in addition' (adding another complaint). Тебя is the object 'you'; же is an emphatic particle stressing the reproach; попрекнут is future 3rd-person plural 'they will reproach (you).' The clause means 'they'll reproach you (on top of that).'
segments.p2-7-g284: Introductory что 'that' begins a reported clause. Даром here means 'for nothing' / 'in vain' (i.e. without right or benefit). Место 'place/spot' and занимаешь (2nd person singular present) 'you occupy' — 'that you take up a place for nothing.'
segments.p2-7-g285: Не скоро помираешь is a short clause: не скоро 'not soon' and помираешь (2nd person present of помирать) literally 'you are dying' but here colloquial for 'you won't die soon' — a reproach that you linger on instead of dying quickly.
segments.p2-7-g286: Infinitive пить ('to drink') functions nominally as the thing desired; не допросишься is a colloquial future 2nd-person reflexive form meaning 'you won't succeed in getting (it) by asking' or more loosely 'you won't get a drink (no matter how you ask).' This is an idiomatic construction where допросишься means to obtain by repeated asking or, contextually, 'you won't get.'
segments.p2-7-g287: С + instrumental (ругательством) indicates manner: 'with abuse/insult.' Подадут is 3rd-person plural future of подать 'they will give/hand/serve (it)' — here 'they'll hand it over' (and do so with abuse).
segments.p2-7-g288: Когда begins the quoted direct question 'When...?' — here functioning as the interrogative 'when.'
segments.p2-7-g289: Дескать is a quotative particle meaning roughly 'they say' or 'so they say,' often used to report someone else's words with an air of contempt or disbelief; it doesn't introduce a factual assertion but the reported tone.
segments.p2-7-g290: Direct address pronoun ты (you) used in the quoted insult; nominative singular you as addressee.
segments.p2-7-g291: Подлячка is an insulting noun — a derogatory term applied to a person ('little scoundrel' / 'mean little creature') and here vocatively addresses the addressee with contempt.
segments.p2-7-g292: Издохнешь is 2nd-person singular future (perfective) of издохнуть: a coarse/vulgar verb meaning 'to croak/die.' It's the direct question 'will you die/croak?'
segments.p2-7-g293: Two present-tense clauses compacted: 'спать мешаешь' — infinitive спать 'to sleep' functions as the thing being hindered and мешаешь (2nd-person present of мешать) 'you hinder/bother' = 'you disturb sleep'; 'стонешь' (2nd-person present) 'you moan.' Both are present-tense reproaches.
segments.p2-7-g294: Гости (guests) is the subject and брезгают is 3rd-person plural present of брезгать meaning 'they recoil in disgust / they are disgusted.' The verb expresses moral/reactionary disgust toward the addressee's behavior.
segments.p2-7-g460: Приду is the 1st person singular future of the perfective verb прийти; the ending -у marks 'I will come'. The initial dash only indicates direct speech; no extra grammatical function.
segments.p2-7-g463: прошептала is the past tense, perfective form with the feminine ending -ла, so it agrees with она (the subject). The verb-before-subject order (прошептала она) is a narrative/inverted word order that stresses the action; решительно is an adverb modifying how she whispered.
segments.p2-7-g464: все ещё is an adverbial phrase meaning 'still'; не + подымая is a negated adverbial participle (деепричастие) from поднимать (imperfective) meaning 'not raising' or 'without raising', describing a simultaneous circumstance. своей головы is a possessive (свой) agreeing with the subject (она); головы is here in the genitive singular (literal 'of her head')—a common stylistic choice with negated participles instead of the plain accusative.
segments.p2-7-g465: А is a discourse conjunction here ('and'/'now then'); теперь is the adverb 'now'; я уйду is the 1st person singular future of the perfective уйти ('I will leave'), with the subject explicitly stated for emphasis before the verb.
segments.p2-7-g466: прощай in this context functions as a farewell formula ('goodbye'/'farewell') rather than a literal imperative meaning 'forgive'. As a standalone utterance it serves as the closing address.
segments.p2-7-g469: до свидания is a fixed farewell phrase literally 'until the (next) meeting': до is a preposition meaning 'until/up to' and governs the genitive, and свидания is the genitive singular of свидание ('meeting'/'rendezvous').
segments.p2-7-g337: И is a simple coordinating conjunction ('and') starting the clause. Ругаться is an infinitive meaning 'to quarrel/argue' that serves as the verb governed by the future plural predicate захотят; the emphatic particle -то attached to ругаться (ругаться-то) highlights or contrasts that action. Из-за тебя is a prepositional phrase meaning 'because of you' (из + за forming the causal preposition 'из‑за'), долго is an adverb modifying the verb phrase, and не захотят is the 3rd person plural future ('they will not want') negated by не.
segments.p2-7-g338: Засыплют is a 3rd person plural future form meaning 'they will cover/fill (up)' (here transitive). Поскорей is an adverb ('more quickly/as soon as possible') modifying засыплют. Мокрой синей глиной is an instrumental phrase ('with wet blue clay') where the adjectives agree in gender, number and case with the feminine noun глиной (instrumental singular) and indicate the material used to cover. The clause then connects with и уйдут ('and they will leave/go'), where уйдут is future 3rd person plural and в кабак ('to the tavern') is a prepositional/accusative directional phrase indicating destination.
segments.p2-7-g341: Тут is an adverb meaning 'here' or 'at this point'; и is a connective often used to add emphasis ('and/so'); конец is the noun 'end' in nominative; твоей памяти is a genitive attributive phrase 'of your memory/remembrance'; на земле is a prepositional phrase 'on earth/among people' specifying the domain.
segments.p2-7-g342: К другим is a dative-phrased contrast 'as for others' (к + dative), дети is the nominative plural subject 'children', на могилу ходят uses ходят in the present habitual sense 'go (regularly/ritually) to the grave' with на + accusative indicating motion to a place.
segments.p2-7-g343: Отцы is a nominative plural noun 'fathers' listed as additional examples of people who visit graves; it stands in apposition to the previous clause.
segments.p2-7-g344: Мужья is another nominative plural noun 'husbands', continuing the list of those who remember the dead.
segments.p2-7-g345: А introduces a contrast ('but/while'); у тебя is a possession/positional idiom ('you have' / 'at you') with у + genitive; ни слезы uses the negative particle ни + noun in the genitive to mean 'not a tear / no tears'.
segments.p2-7-g346: Ни вздоха repeats the negative pattern (ни + genitive) to add 'nor a sigh'; the genitive singular вздоха is used because of the negation.
segments.p2-7-g347: Ни поминания continues the enumeration with ни + genitive: 'nor remembrance/mention' (поминания meaning commemoration or being remembered).
segments.p2-7-g348: И просто вводит ещё одну мысль ('and'); никто is the pronoun 'no one'; the enclitic -то here (никто-то) is emphatic/colloquial, adding rhetorical stress ('no one, mind you').
segments.p2-7-g349: Повторение никто-то (again 'no one' with the emphatic -то) intensifies the absoluteness of the statement by repetition.
segments.p2-7-g350: Никогда ... не придет is a negated temporal/universal clause: никогда ('never') with the future придет ('will come') negated by не, so 'will never come'. В целом мире is a locative/prepositional phrase 'in the whole world' (в + adjective in prepositional + мире), and к тебе is the dative/directional 'to you'.
segments.p2-7-g351: Имя твое is the nominative subject 'your name'; исчезнет is 3rd person singular future 'will disappear/vanish'; с лица земли is an idiomatic prepositional phrase ('from the face of the earth') with с meaning 'from' and лица in the genitive (face), земли 'earth' in genitive; так links to the following comparative/explanatory clause ('so/thus').
segments.p2-7-g352: Как бы introduces a counterfactual comparison 'as if'; бы is the conditional/optative particle used to mark the hypothetical flavor; совсем 'completely' intensifies the negation; тебя is the accusative of 'you' used impersonally with бывало and рождалось; никогда не бывало is an impersonal past construction meaning 'had never been' or 'never existed', and и не рождалось repeats the negation with the imperfective past/passive‑reflexive рождалось 'been born' — together giving 'as if you had never existed and had never been born.'
segments.p2-7-g353: A short, verbless exclamation made of two nouns joined by да. Here да has the archaic/conjunctive sense 'and' (used idiomatically to mean 'nothing but' or 'only'); the phrase emphasizes utter dreariness ('mud and swamp'). Word order is nominative nouns in apposition.
segments.p2-7-g354: Хоть + verb expresses concession ('even if' / 'no matter how much'). Стучи is a 2nd-person singular imperative (colloquial) meaning 'knock' (here used generically, not addressed to a particular person). Себе is a colloquial intensifier/reflexive particle ('for yourself' / 'to yourself') that softens or colors the imperative; там = 'there'; по ночам = 'at nights' (по + dative plural expresses repeated time).
segments.p2-7-g355: Когда introduces a temporal clause 'when'. Мертвецы is plural nominative 'the dead' (collective). Встают is present-tense indicative (or a general/present-tense use) 'rise/get up'—used here of the dead rising at night.
segments.p2-7-g356: В + accusative (в крышу) indicates direction/target: 'at/against' the coffin lid (i.e., to beat on it). Гробовую is the adjective 'funereal' modifying крышу (coffin lid); крышу is accusative singular of крыша ('roof/lid') used literally of a coffin's lid.
segments.p2-7-g357: Пустите is the imperative plural (from пущать/пустить) addressing people: 'let (us) out' / 'allow (us)'. In direct speech it functions as a plea/command to those being addressed.
segments.p2-7-g358: Добрые люди is a vocative-like address in the nominative: 'good people' or 'kind people'—добрые is the plural adjective, люди the plural noun. The comma shows direct address.
segments.p2-7-g359: На свет пожить is an idiomatic phrase: на + noun expresses direction/purpose ('to the light' / 'into the world'), свет here means 'light' or 'the world' (i.e., living openly), and пожить is the perfective/iterative infinitive meaning 'to live for a while' / 'to have a bit of life'—together 'to be let out for a little while to live in the light.'
segments.p2-7-g378: Давно уже предчувствовал я places two adverbials before the past-tense verb for emphasis: давно уже (an adverbial phrase, ‘for a long time/already’) + предчувствовал (past tense, masculine singular) and then the pronoun я is placed after the verb as a stylistic inversion. The verb предчувствовал is past indicative expressing a state the speaker had prior to the narrative moment.
segments.p2-7-g379: что introduces a subordinate clause ('that ...'). Перевернул and разбил are perfective past-tense verbs, masculine singular, with the implied subject я: both are completed actions attributed to the speaker. Всю modifies душу (accusative) to indicate totality; the two verbs are coordinated by и and share the same direct-object structure.
segments.p2-7-g380: И is a coordinating conjunction linking the subordinate clause just finished to the comparative clause that follows; here it functions simply as 'and'.
segments.p2-7-g381: Чем больше ... is the first half of a correlative comparative construction (the more ...). Чем introduces the comparative clause; больше is the comparative adverb. Удостоверялся is past imperfective (becoming or being convinced over time) and takes the prepositional phrase в том ('in that/of that'), where том refers back to the content of the preceding clause.
segments.p2-7-g382: Тем больше completes the correlative ('the more ... the more ...'). Желал is past tense 'wanted'; it governs the infinitive достигнуть. Поскорее and как можно сильнее are adverbial degree modifiers: поскорее 'sooner/as quickly as possible', как можно сильнее 'as strongly/forcefully as possible'. Цели is the object (genitive after достигнуть).
segments.p2-7-g383: Игра in nominative here is used as a heading-like noun or topic word; repeated in the next segment for emphasis. It is feminine and stands alone for effect.
segments.p2-7-g384: игра (subject) + увлекла (past tense, perfective, feminine to agree with игра) + меня (accusative object). Увлекла means 'carried away/entranced/captured' — a simple S-V-O clause.
segments.p2-7-g385: Впрочем is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'however' or 'moreover', signaling a qualification or addition.
segments.p2-7-g386: Не одна игра is an elliptical phrase meaning 'not a single game' or more idiomatically 'not the only one' — не (negation) + одна (one) + игра (noun). The clause is elliptical (the verb is omitted) and functions to correct/expand the previous statement.
segments.p2-7-g389: Я знал is a simple past-tense main clause: I knew. Знал is the masculine past indicative of знать.
segments.p2-7-g390: Что introduces the content clause describing how he spoke. Говорю is present-tense first-person (habitual/present characteristic) used inside a past narration (a common Russian stylistic device: 'I knew that I speak...'). Туго is an adverb meaning 'awkwardly/with difficulty' describing the manner of speaking.
segments.p2-7-g391: Выделанно is an adverb meaning 'affectedly' or 'mannered'; it's another manner adverb modifying говорю and is presented in a list of qualities.
segments.p2-7-g392: Даже is an emphatic adverb 'even', and книжно is an adverb meaning 'bookishly' or 'in a bookish style', continuing the list of manner adverbs.
segments.p2-7-g393: Одним словом is an idiomatic linking phrase ('in a word' / 'to sum up') in the instrumental case (одним словом). It introduces the summary that follows.
segments.p2-7-g394: Я иначе и не умел is a compact past-tense expression meaning 'I didn't know how to do otherwise' or 'I couldn't do it differently.' Умел (past of уметь) expresses ability/skill; the particles иначе 'otherwise' and и (here adding emphasis) combine with не to negate that ability.
segments.p2-7-g395: Как introduces a comparative/quotative clause giving the quoted formula. Точно по книжке is an idiomatic phrase: точно 'exactly' + по книжке 'by the book/according to the book' (по + dative/locative form книжке), i.e. 'exactly by the book.' The quoted phrase is a glib formula describing his manner.
segments.p2-7-g396: Но это не смущало меня is a past-tense negative clause: но (but), это (this — neuter subject), не (not), смущало (past imperfective 'embarrassed/disturbed'), меня (accusative). The verb agrees with the neuter subject это.
segments.p2-7-g397: Я ведь знал: я is the subject, ведь is a modal/particle meaning 'after all' or 'you see' adding a justificatory tone, and знал is past tense 'knew' — a reminder or reinforcement of his confidence.
segments.p2-7-g398: Предчувствовал is another past-tense verb (I had a presentiment/anticipated) presented as an additional assertion; the comma-separated placement emphasizes that this was an inner conviction as well as knowledge.
segments.p2-7-g399: The subordinate content clause begins with что ('that'). Меня поймут is future-tense (поймут, 3rd-person plural) meaning 'they will understand me.' The second что introduces another clause: самая эта книжность is emphatic (самая ... эта = 'this very bookishness'), может is a modal 'may/could', and подспорить делу is an infinitive phrase where подспорить (perfective infinitive) takes делу in the dative: 'to give support to the matter/assist the cause.' Ещё больше is an intensifier 'even more.'
segments.p2-7-g409: Все modifies тело and here means 'the whole/entire'; молодое is an adjective 'young/youthful' agreeing with тело (neuter); тело is the grammatical subject (neuter singular). Ее is a possessive pronoun 'her' placed after the noun for stylistic emphasis. Вздрагивало is the past imperfective neuter singular of вздрагивать (agreeing with the neuter subject тело) and conveys an ongoing or repeated trembling ('was trembling').
segments.p2-7-g410: Как is the comparative particle 'as/like' here introducing a simile. В + prepositional plural судорогах gives 'in convulsions/spasms' (an idiomatic locative phrase describing the manner of trembling).
segments.p2-7-g411: Спершиеся is a past participle (verbal adjective) from спереться meaning 'having gathered/been held back' and modifies рыдания (plural). В груди is a locative phrase 'in (her) chest'. Рыдания is nominative plural (the subject of the clause). Теснили is the past plural imperfective of теснить, agreeing with plural рыдания, meaning 'pressed/crowded'.
segments.p2-7-g412: Рвали is past plural imperfective of рвать ('to tear') and takes the object ее ('her'), so 'tore at her' (continuous/past action). И вдруг is 'and suddenly'; воплями is instrumental plural ('by/with shrieks') indicating the manner in which the action proceeds (it links into the next segment).
segments.p2-7-g413: Криками is another instrumental plural noun parallel to воплями ('with cries'), marking manner. Вырвались is the past plural reflexive/intransitive verb вырываться here meaning 'burst out' or 'broke free' (agreeing with plural рыдания). Наружу is an adverb 'outwards/outside', completing the motion.
segments.p2-7-g414: Тогда is a temporal adverb 'then/at that moment'. Еще сильнее is a comparative phrase 'even more strongly/more closely' (еще intensifies the comparative сильнее). Приникала is past imperfective feminine singular of приникать 'to press/lean close', agreeing with она; the verb-before-pronoun order (приникала она) gives a slightly emphatic or narrative tone. К подушке uses the dative to indicate direction/toward the pillow.
segments.p2-7-g415: Ей is the dative form 'to her' used with the impersonal construction хотелось; ей не хотелось literally 'to her it did not feel like' — idiomatically 'she did not want'. Не is the negation and хотелось is the past-form impersonal verb 'wanted/felt like' (here negated).
segments.p2-7-g416: Чтобы introduces a subordinate clause expressing the content of what she did not want ('that/so that'). Кто-нибудь is an indefinite pronoun 'anyone' (кто + нибудь). Здесь is the locative adverb 'here', specifying the place where she does not want anyone to know.
segments.p2-7-g417: Хоть (here) functions as an emphasizer meaning roughly 'not even'/'not a single' when paired with одна живая душа — 'a single living soul' (одна agrees with feminine душа). Узнала is past feminine singular of узнать 'to find out/learn' (so 'to learn about'). Про + accusative marks the content 'about her torment and tears' (ее терзание и слезы); ее is possessive 'her'.
segments.p2-7-g418: Она is the subject 'she'. Кусала is past imperfective feminine singular of кусать ('to bite') implying repeated or ongoing action ('was biting' or 'kept biting'). Подушку is the accusative object 'the pillow'.
segments.p2-7-g419: Прокусила is past perfective feminine singular of прокусить 'to bite through' or 'to bite so as to cause bleeding' — a completed action relative to кусала. Руку свою places the possessive after the noun for emphasis ('her hand'). В кровь is an idiom meaning 'to the point of blood' ('until it bled'). The parenthesis '(я видел это потом)' is a narrator's aside: я видел это потом = 'I saw this later'. Или is the coordinating conjunction 'or' introducing an alternative action.
segments.p2-7-g420: Вцепившись is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) formed from вцепиться, meaning 'having clutched' or 'clutching' and sets a concomitant manner for the main verb. Пальцами is instrumental plural 'with her fingers' (the means). В свои распутавшиеся косы uses в + accusative to indicate gripping 'into her own loosened/untied braids'; свои is the reflexive possessive referring back to the subject, распутавшиеся is a past participle modifying косы ('untangled/loosened braids').
segments.p2-7-g421: Так и is an idiomatic combination emphasizing the continued/resulting state ('and thus/and so'); замирала is past imperfective feminine singular of замирать 'to freeze/stop moving' (habitual or repeated past action here). В усилии uses the preposition в with the noun усилии (locative/instrumental sense) to express 'in the effort' or 'from the exertion' of holding back.
segments.p2-7-g422: Сдерживая and стискивая are present adverbial participles (деепричастия) describing simultaneous actions ('holding back' and 'clenching'). Дыхание and зубы are the objects of those gerunds ('holding her breath' and 'clenching her teeth'). The pairing of two gerunds describes the manner in which she froze.
segments.p2-7-g295: Это is a neuter demonstrative pronoun used as a dummy subject meaning 'that/it' (here 'that's'); верно is a short-form predicative (adverbial/short adjective) meaning 'true/correct' and functions as the main predicate without an explicit copula.
segments.p2-7-g296: я сам uses сам as an emphatic reflexive/enphatic element ('I myself'); подслушал is perfective past tense (1st person singular) meaning 'overheard/eavesdropped on'; такие слова is the direct object 'such words/remarks' with такие qualifying слова.
segments.p2-7-g297: Сунут is the 3rd-person-plural future (perfective) of сунуть, colloquial for 'they will shove/put'; the subject is omitted (impersonal 'they/people'); тебя is the accusative object 'you'.
segments.p2-7-g298: издыхающую is an accusative feminine singular participle (or adjectival participle) of издыхать, describing the state of 'you' — 'you, expiring/dying'. Its case and gender agree with тебя.
segments.p2-7-g299: The first в (into/toward) with самый + угол expresses motion into a specific place ('into the very foul corner'); самый is an intensifier 'the very'; смрадный is an attributive adjective 'foul-smelling'; the second в governs подвале in the prepositional (locative) — 'in the cellar'.
segments.p2-7-g300: Темень is a nominative noun used as a short exclamatory comment ('darkness'/'gloom'), set off by a dash like an appositive description of the place.
segments.p2-7-g301: Сырость is another nominative noun in the same appositive list ('dampness'), giving additional harsh qualities of the corner; the terse nominative nouns form a graphic catalogue.
segments.p2-7-g302: Что here introduces a rhetorical question ('what/what then') addressing ты; it functions as an interrogative/exclamatory particle; ты is the nominative subject 'you'.
segments.p2-7-g303: Лежа is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'lying' (describing a concurrent circumstance); the enclitic particle то after the gerund (лежа-то) adds colloquial emphasis/contrast; одна is an adjective in the nominative feminine meaning 'alone', modifying the implied subject.
segments.p2-7-g304: Тогда is an adverb 'then/in that case'; передумаешь is 2nd-person singular future of передумать (perfective) 'will change your mind' — placed at the end as the main verb of the rhetorical question.
segments.p2-7-g305: Помрешь is the 2nd-person singular future (perfective) of помереть/pомрать 'you will die'; it stands alone as the opening clause describing the fate.
segments.p2-7-g306: Соберут is 3rd-person plural future (perfective) of собрать 'they will gather/collect'; наскоро is an adverb 'hastily' modifying the verb.
segments.p2-7-g307: Чужой рукой uses чужой (another's/foreign) plus рукой in the instrumental to indicate the agent/instrument ('by someone else's hand'/'with someone else's hands'), i.e. strangers handling the body.
segments.p2-7-g308: С + ворчаньем uses the preposition с with an instrumental noun to express manner: 'with grumbling' (how they act as they gather the body).
segments.p2-7-g309: С + нетерпением is the same instrumental construction: 'with impatience' or 'eagerly' — another manner adverbial qualifying their behavior.
segments.p2-7-g310: Никто-то combines никто ('no one') with the particle -то, which here adds colloquial emphasis; не negates the verb благословит (3rd sing. future 'will bless') and тебя is the object — 'no one will bless you.'
segments.p2-7-g311: Again никто-то (emphatic 'no one') + не + вздохнет (3rd sing. future 'will sigh'); the preposition по with dative тебя (по тебе) expresses the object of the emotion here: 'no one will sigh for you/over you.'
segments.p2-7-g312: Только бы ... is a colloquial optative construction expressing the wish/desire ('they only wish that ...'); бы is the modal/optative particle; поскорей is an adverb 'as quickly as possible'; с плеч долой is an idiomatic phrase with с indicating 'from/off' and долой meaning 'away with/off' — 'they just want you off their shoulders.'
segments.p2-7-g313: Купят is 3rd-person plural future (perfective) of купить 'they will buy'; колоду is the accusative of колода (a block/board/log) and here means a plank/board used as a bier — direct object.
segments.p2-7-g314: Вынесут is 3rd-person plural future (perfective) of вынести 'they will carry out/bring out' and continues the sequence of actions begun by купят.
segments.p2-7-g315: Как introduces a comparative/example clause 'as'; сегодня 'today' locates the example in time; ту is the feminine demonstrative in accusative 'that one' referring back to a specific person mentioned or shown.
segments.p2-7-g316: Бедную is an accusative feminine adjective 'the poor (one)' modifying ту — 'that poor woman/person' — completing the reference in the comparative clause.
segments.p2-7-g317: Выносили is the past plural imperfective of выносить 'they carried out' — it describes the completed past action (how they handled that other person) and agrees with the implied plural subject.
segments.p2-7-g318: В кабак is a directional phrase ('into the tavern'); поминать is an infinitive 'to commemorate/hold memorial rites' expressing purpose, and пойдут is 3rd-person plural future of пойти 'they will go' — together 'they will go to the tavern to hold a memorial.'
segments.p2-7-g441: Я сел подле нее и взял ее руки; — 'Я сел' is past tense, masculine singular of сесть ('I sat down'). 'Подле' governs the genitive ('нее') and means 'beside/near'. The conjunction 'и' links the two coordinated past actions; 'взял' is past masculine of взять ('took'), and 'ее руки' is the accusative plural 'her hands'. The semicolon separates this action from the immediate reaction that follows.
segments.p2-7-g442: она опомнилась, — Subject 'она' + past reflexive perfective 'опомнилась' (опомниться) means 'she came to her senses / recovered herself'. The short clause and comma mark a quick recovery of consciousness or composure.
segments.p2-7-g443: бросилась ко мне, — 'Броситься' in past reflexive form expresses a sudden motion ('threw herself'/'rushed'); 'ко' is the form of preposition 'к' used before 'мне', which is dative ('to me'). The whole phrase means 'she rushed toward me' or 'sprang at me'.
segments.p2-7-g444: хотела было обхватить меня, — 'Хотела' is past feminine of хотеть ('wanted'); the particle 'было' after the verb signals an attempted or aborted action ('was about to / tried to'). 'Обхватить' is the perfective infinitive 'to embrace/encircle', and 'меня' is the accusative object 'me'.
segments.p2-7-g445: но не посмела и тихо наклонила передо мной голову. — 'Но' ('but') contrasts the expectation; 'не посмела' is past feminine of посметь ('did not dare'). The conjunction 'и' links the following action: 'тихо наклонила' (past feminine of наклонить) 'quietly bowed/leaned'; 'передо' (from перед) governs the pronoun in the instrumental/oblique form 'мной' to mean 'before me / in my presence'; 'голову' is the accusative 'head'.
segments.p2-7-g446: - Лиза, — A direct vocative/address: the name 'Лиза' is used to call the addressee. The dash plus comma mark the pause of direct address.
segments.p2-7-g447: друг мой, — 'Друг' ('friend') followed by the possessive 'мой' ('my') forms an affectionate apposition 'my friend'; word order noun + possessive is normal in Russian for this idiomatic phrase.
segments.p2-7-g448: я напрасно. — 'Я' is the subject pronoun 'I'; 'напрасно' is an adverb meaning 'in vain' or 'to no avail'. The clause is elliptical (no copula), and here means roughly 'what I say is in vain' or 'it is useless for me'.
segments.p2-7-g451: ты прости меня, — 'Ты' (informal 'you') precedes the imperative 'прости' (from простить) addressing the listener directly: 'forgive me'. Placing 'ты' before the imperative is emphatic/familiar; 'меня' is the accusative object 'me'.
segments.p2-7-g452: - начал было я, — 'Начал' (past masculine of начать) 'I began'; the particle 'было' after the verb indicates the beginning was momentary or about to happen but was then interrupted. Word order verb–particle–pronoun (начал было я) is a slight inversion common in narrative to show interruption.
segments.p2-7-g453: - но она сжала в своих пальцах мои руки с такою силою, — 'Но' ('but') contrasts the intended speech; 'она сжала' is past feminine of сжать ('squeezed/clenched'). 'В своих пальцах' (preposition + possessive reflexive) literally 'in her own fingers' uses the locative/oblique plural 'пальцах'. 'Мои руки' is the object 'my hands'. 'С такою силою' (archaic/poetic instrumental) 'with such force' modifies the verb; 'такою' is an older form of 'такой', and 'силою' is the instrumental of 'сила' 'by force/with force'.
segments.p2-7-g454: что я догадался, — 'Что' introduces the result clause ('that'); 'я догадался' is past masculine of догадаться, here meaning 'I guessed' or better 'I realized/inferred'. This clause reports the narrator's immediate inference about his speech.
segments.p2-7-g455: что не то говорю, — Here 'что' again introduces the content: 'that'. 'Не то' is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'not the right thing' / 'something wrong/inappropriate'. 'Говорю' is present tense 'I say' used in the subordinate content clause to express the content of his speech ('that I am saying the wrong thing').
segments.p2-7-g456: и перестал. — 'И' ('and') links to the final action; 'перестал' is past masculine of перестать ('stopped/ceased'), here meaning he stopped speaking or stopped his attempted speech.
segments.p2-7-g457: Вот is a demonstrative/introductory particle meaning 'here/there' used to present something. Мой адрес is a simple nominative noun phrase 'my address' functioning as what is being shown; the comma marks the pause before addressing the person.
segments.p2-7-g458: Лиза is the addressee's name set off by commas; Russian lacks a special vocative case, so the name simply appears in the nominative to indicate whom the speaker addresses.
segments.p2-7-g459: Приходи is the informal singular imperative of приходить ('come'). Ко is the allomorph of the preposition к used before certain pronouns/words for euphony, and мне is the dative pronoun 'to me' — ко мне = 'to my place / to me'.
segments.p2-7-g601: This is an exclamatory noun phrase made of an adjective + noun. Гадкая is the full-form adjective in the nominative feminine singular (matching истина) placed before the noun and carrying a pejorative, colloquial sense 'nasty/disgusting'. Истина is a feminine nominative singular noun; as a standalone exclamation it names the truth (Russian has no article or copula here), so English must supply 'what a' or 'the' to render the force of the exclamation.
segments.p2-7-g487: Убегая is a present gerund (деепричастие) from убегать meaning “while/as she was running away”; it expresses an action simultaneous to the main clause and attaches to the subject of the sentence.
segments.p2-7-g488: она — subject pronoun 'she'. вся here is an intensifying word meaning 'entirely/all over' modifying the following predicate. покраснела is past perfective (покраснеть) meaning 'flushed/turned red' (completed action) and agrees with the feminine subject.
segments.p2-7-g489: глаза (nominative plural) is the subject for the verb блестели; ее is the possessive/attributive 'her' placed after 'глаза'. блестели is the imperfective past plural of блестеть, 'were shining/gleaming', describing a continuing state.
segments.p2-7-g490: на + locative plural губах gives the location 'on (her) lips'. показалась is the past tense (feminine in form, but here used impersonally with улыбка as subject) of показаться 'appeared'. улыбка is the feminine noun 'smile' and is the syntactic subject of показалась.
segments.p2-7-g491: Что бы такое? is an exclamatory, elliptical question. что 'what' with the particle бы softens/hypotheticalizes the question ('what could it be?'), and такое ('such a thing/that sort of thing') refers vaguely to whatever prompted her expression.
segments.p2-7-g492: Я is 'I'. поневоле is an adverb meaning 'involuntarily/against my will' modifying the action. дождался is the past perfective of дождаться ('to wait (for)') and here means 'I ended up waiting' or 'I couldn't help waiting' — completed action by the speaker.
segments.p2-7-g493: она 'she'. воротилась is past tense (feminine) of воротиться 'to return' (here: 'came back'). через + минуту is a temporal phrase meaning 'after a minute' (через used with a time span meaning 'in/after (time)'); минуту is accusative indicating the span.
segments.p2-7-g494: со is the variant of с used before certain consonant clusters; взглядом is the instrumental singular of взгляд and means 'with a look' — the instrumental expresses accompaniment/manner.
segments.p2-7-g495: как будто is a fixed phrase 'as if / as though'. бросившим is the past active participle (full form in the instrumental) of бросить and is placed in the instrumental to agree with взглядом; it means 'having thrown/cast' and here modifies the look. прощения is the genitive of прощение 'forgiveness' (object of бросившим), and за что-то means 'for something' (за meaning 'for' here).
segments.p2-7-g496: Вообще is an adverb 'in general / on the whole' setting the comment's scope. это is a demonstrative/placeholder 'it/this'. уже было is past 'already was'. не то лицо is an idiomatic negation 'not the same face' (то here = 'the former/the same as before'). лицо is the noun 'face'.
segments.p2-7-g497: не тот взгляд repeats the contrast: не (not) + тот (that/the same) + взгляд (look/gaze), meaning 'not the same look'. This parallels the previous nominal phrase.
segments.p2-7-g498: как давеча is a comparative/time phrase 'as (it was) the other day' or 'as recently' with давеча meaning 'recently/the other day'.
segments.p2-7-g499: угрюмый is an adjective 'sullen/gloomy' presented here as an appositive description of the face/look; the dash marks the shift to these descriptive adjectives.
segments.p2-7-g500: недоверчивый 'distrustful/suspicious' and упорный 'persistent/stubborn' are coordinate adjectives completing the list describing the earlier face/look; и is the coordinating conjunction 'and'.
segments.p2-7-g501: Взгляд 'gaze/look' is the subject. теперь 'now' places it temporally. ее is the possessive 'her'. был is the past tense of 'to be' linking to the nominal predicate. просящий is a present participle/adjectival form of просить meaning 'asking/pleading' and functions as the predicate adjective.
segments.p2-7-g502: мягкий is an adjective 'soft/gentle' continuing the series of predicate adjectives describing the gaze.
segments.p2-7-g503: а вместе с тем is a phrase meaning 'and at the same time / yet'; a is the simple conjunction 'and/but', вместе с тем = 'together with that/at the same time'. доверчивый is an adjective 'trusting/credulous', adding a simultaneous quality to the gaze.
segments.p2-7-g504: ласковый is an adjective 'affectionate/gentle', another coordinated descriptor of the gaze.
segments.p2-7-g505: робкий is the final adjective in the series, meaning 'timid' or 'shy', completing the nuanced portrait of the look.
segments.p2-7-g560: И вот functions as a narrative/discourse marker meaning 'and so' or 'and then' (вот emphasizes immediacy). На другой день после танцев is a time phrase placed up front ('on the next day after the dances/ball'); the parenthetical (три дня назад) restates that time as 'three days ago'. Он и прислал uses the past perfective прислал to mark a completed action; the particle и before the verb adds slight emphasis ('indeed/even'), and через + noun marks the means/agent of delivery ('through/by means of a female friend').
segments.p2-7-g561: С которой introduces a relative clause ('with whom') and grammatically matches the instrumental case required by the preposition с. Она is the subject of the clause; на вечер here marks the occasion/destination ('for the evening' / 'to spend the evening'), and ездила is the past imperfective of ездить indicating she went/rode there (in 19th-century Russian implying a trip by carriage or that she went to spend the evening).
segments.p2-7-g562: Это письмо is the direct object placed after the relative clause; это is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' (emphatic identification), and письмо is the accusative of the neuter noun 'letter' (form identical to nominative).
segments.p2-7-g565: A single coordinating conjunction и used here as a trailing, elliptical remark — in context it functions as a short connective/exclamatory 'and' that implies continuation or emphasis rather than introducing a full clause.
segments.p2-7-g568: Ну вот и все is a set phrase used to close a story: ну and вот are discourse particles ('well' / 'now'), и here links to the resulting conclusion ('and so'), and все is the predicate nominal meaning 'all'/'that's all'. The whole phrase functions as 'Well, that's all.'
segments.p2-7-g569: Она is the subject pronoun 'she'. Как-то is a colloquial adverbial phrase ('somehow' / 'in a somewhat ... way') formed from как + то and here modifies the manner; стыдливо is an adverb 'bashfully'. Опустила is the past feminine singular of опустить ('lowered'), taking a direct object; свои is the reflexive possessive 'her own' agreeing with the object, and сверкавшие is a past active participle 'sparkling' modifying глаза (eyes).
segments.p2-7-g570: Когда introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when'. Кончила is past feminine of кончить meaning 'finished' (not 'died' here), and рассказывать is the imperfective infinitive 'to tell' — together кончила рассказывать = 'finished telling'. The subject is understood from the main clause (она).
segments.p2-7-g423: 'Я было начал что-то говорить ей,' — 'было' here is the particle used with a past tense verb to mark an action that was beginning or attempted (often with the sense 'had begun / was about to') and may be cut short; 'что-то' is the indefinite pronoun 'something' (что + -то). Word order is subject (Я) + discourse-particle + past verb + object phrase.
segments.p2-7-g424: 'просить ее успокоиться,' — a continuation of what he was beginning to do: two infinitives in sequence (просить ... успокоиться) express purpose/intent (to ask/beg her to calm down). The object pronoun 'ее' is the person being asked (accusative/infinitival object).
segments.p2-7-g425: 'но почувствовал,' — simple past (perfective) 'I felt/perceived'; it introduces the speaker's change of feeling and leads into a subordinate clause. The conjunction 'но' contrasts this sudden feeling with his previous intention.
segments.p2-7-g426: 'что не смею,' — 'что' introduces the content clause ('that ...'); 'не смею' is present tense of сметь ('dare') used in a past narration to report the speaker's immediate feeling ('I felt that I did not dare' — Russian often uses the present to express that sense).
segments.p2-7-g427: 'и вдруг сам,' — 'и' links the sudden change; 'вдруг' = 'suddenly'; 'сам' is emphatic/reflexive 'myself' (emphasis that he himself acted). Together they mark a sudden, self-initiated movement.
segments.p2-7-g428: 'весь в каком-то ознобе,' — 'весь' = 'completely/entirely'; 'в' + locative 'ознобе' = 'in a chill/shiver'; 'каком-то' is indefinite ('some kind of'), so the phrase means 'completely in some sort of chill/shivering'.
segments.p2-7-g429: 'почти в ужасе,' — 'почти' = 'almost'; 'в ужасе' is a set expression meaning 'in terror/terror-struck.' It intensifies his physical state following 'весь в ознобе.'
segments.p2-7-g430: 'бросился ощупью,' — 'бросился' is perfective past of броситься, here meaning 'hurried/ threw himself to do something'; 'ощупью' is an adverbial form meaning 'by feeling/with his hands' (groping). Together: 'he rushed, groping (about)'.
segments.p2-7-g431: 'кое-как наскоро сбираться в дорогу.' — 'кое-как' = 'in a makeshift / somehow' manner; it combines with 'наскоро' ('hastily') to modify the verb; 'сбираться' (imperfective infinitive) = 'to get ready/prepare' and here expresses beginning to prepare; 'в дорогу' (accusative) is the set phrase 'to set out / to go on the road.' The imperfective infinitive after бросился indicates he set about beginning that action in haste.
segments.p2-7-g432: 'Было темно:' — impersonal past construction: 'было' + predicate adverb 'темно' = 'it was dark.' The colon links this background condition to what follows.
segments.p2-7-g433: 'как ни старался я,' — fixed concessive construction 'как ни + past' meaning 'however (much) I tried'; word order (verb before subject) is common in Russian subordinate concessive clauses.
segments.p2-7-g434: 'но не мог кончить скоро.' — 'но' = 'but'; 'не мог' is past tense of мочь ('could not') expressing inability; 'кончить' is perfective infinitive 'to finish' and 'скоро' qualifies how soon/quickly — 'could not finish quickly.'
segments.p2-7-g435: Whole segment: 'Вдруг я ощупал коробку спичек и подсвечник с цельной непочатой свечой.' — 'вдруг' = 'suddenly'; 'ощупал' (perfective, transitive past) means 'felt with his hands / groped and found'; 'коробку спичек' and 'подсвечник' are direct objects; 'с цельной непочатой свечой' is an instrumental phrase qualifying the candlestick ('with a whole/unopened candle').
segments.p2-7-g436: 'Только лишь свет озарил комнату,' — 'только лишь' functions as a temporal connector 'no sooner / as soon as'; 'свет озарил комнату' is a perfective past clause 'the light illuminated the room.' The phrase sets the immediate temporal background for the following actions.
segments.p2-7-g437: 'Лиза вдруг вскочила,' — proper name + 'вдруг' = 'suddenly'; 'вскочила' is perfective past of 'вскочить' meaning 'started up / sprang up' (a quick movement from a seated or lying position).
segments.p2-7-g438: 'села и с каким-то искривленным лицом,' — 'села' is the past feminine form of 'сесть' ('sat down' / 'sat'); here it follows 'вскочила' to record her movements. 'с каким-то искривленным лицом' is an ablative/instrumental descriptive phrase 'with some distorted/twisted expression on her face.'
segments.p2-7-g439: 'с полусумасшедшей улыбкой,' — another instrumental descriptive phrase: 'with a half-mad / semi-crazed smile' modifying how she looked at him; 'полусумасшедшей' is a compound adjective, 'улыбкой' is instrumental of 'smile.'
segments.p2-7-g440: 'почти бессмысленно посмотрела на меня.' — 'почти' = 'almost'; 'бессмысленно' = 'senselessly / blankly'; 'посмотрела' is perfective past of 'посмотреть' ('looked'); 'на меня' marks the object of the look. Together: 'looked at me almost without meaning / almost blankly.'
segments.p2-7-g591: Это безличная конструкция с дательным «мне» + прошедшим формой глагола «хотелось» (имперсональная форма от хотеться), означающая «мне хотелось» = «я чувствовал желание». «Так» — наречие степени, усиливающее желание. Инфинитив «уйти» является дополнением и выражает действие, к которому было это желание.
segments.p2-7-g594: «Я прошел» — прошедшее время, мужской род, одушевлённый субъект «я»; глагол пройти здесь значит «пройти/пройти путь, пройти расстояние» (cover a distance). «Всю дорогу» — винительный падеж, прямое дополнение «весь» в форме всю (fem. because дорогу) = «весь путь». «Пешком» — наречное выражение образа действия, стоящее после дополнения.
segments.p2-7-g595: Фраза «несмотря на то, что...» — устойчивый вводный союз, где «несмотря» = «despite», «на то» — часть конструкции; вместе вводят уступочное придаточное предложение. После этой части обязательно следует «что» + придаточное.
segments.p2-7-g596: Придаточное, вводимое «что», имеет подлежащее «мокрый снег» (им.п.) и глагол прош. времени «валил» в несов. виде, указывающий на продолжающееся действие («was falling»). «Всё ещё» — сочетание наречий, означающее «still» (ещё с усилителем всё). «Хлопьями» стоит в творительном падеже и обозначает способ/вид (падая «хлопьями» = in flakes).
segments.p2-7-g597: «Я был измучен» — сочетание связки в прошедшем времени «был» и краткого страдательного причастия/краткого прилагательного «измучен», обозначающее состояние (I was exhausted). Такая конструкция подчёркивает завершённость или факт состояния в прошлом.
segments.p2-7-g598: Краткое страдательное причастие «раздавлен» следует параллельно предыдущему члену и указывает на ещё одно состояние: буквально «crushed», здесь в переносном смысле — «overwhelmed/crushed (emotionally/mentally)». Форма краткая, согласована по роду с «я» (м.р.).
segments.p2-7-g599: Предложный оборот «в недоумении» (предлог «в» + существительное в предложном падеже) выражает состояние «in perplexity / bewilderment». Это завершает перечисление состояний рассказчика.
segments.p2-7-g600: «Но» — противительный союз. «Истина» — подлежащее в именительном падеже; «уже» — обстоятельство времени «already». «Сверкала» — глагол прошедшего времени несовершенного вида, переносно «was gleaming/was flashing» (truth beginning to reveal itself). «Из-за недоумения» функционирует как причинно-источниковый оборот: здесь «из-за» (составной предлог «из» + «за») означает «out of/because of», а «недоумения» стоит в родительном падеже; вместе — «из-за недоумения» = «out of/because of bewilderment».
segments.p2-7-g506: Так is an adverb modifying the verb and means 'so/like that'; смотрят is the 3rd-person plural present of смотреть (the main verb 'look'); дети is the nominative plural subject. На тех is a prepositional phrase with на + the demonstrative pronoun (тех = 'those') indicating the target of the looking ('at those').
segments.p2-7-g507: Кого begins a relative clause (accusative of кто) 'whom/that they...', очень любят is a verb phrase (3rd pl present) meaning 'love very much'. И links the coordinated predicate; у кого — 'from whom' uses the preposition у with the wh-pronoun in genitive to mark the person from whom something is asked; чего-нибудь is the genitive of что + нибудь forming an indefinite 'something/anything' (genitive required by просить); просят is 3rd-plural present 'they ask/request/beg'.
segments.p2-7-g508: Глаза is the noun 'eyes' (subject). У ней были is the possessive construction 'she had' (у + pronoun in genitive + past of быть), so literally 'eyes at her were'; светло-карие is a compound adjective formed from the adverb светло + adjective карие meaning 'light-brown' and agrees with глаза.
segments.p2-7-g509: Прекрасные глаза is an appositive description repeating 'eyes' for emphasis; прекрасные is plural nominative agreeing with глаза and means 'beautiful' or 'splendid'.
segments.p2-7-g510: Живые is an adjectival predicate (plural nominative) describing the eyes as 'alive' or 'lively'; it is set off by commas as a coordinated list of qualities.
segments.p2-7-g511: Уме́вшие is a past active participle (plural) modifying глаза: 'able/knowing how to'. It takes the infinitive отразить ('to reflect') and the pronominal phrase в себе ('in themselves') as its complement; и introduces the first coordinated object любовь ('love').
segments.p2-7-g512: И connects the second coordinated object. Угрюмую ненависть is in the accusative (feminine) as the object of отразить and means 'sullen/gloomy hatred'; the adjective угрюмую agrees with ненависть.
segments.p2-7-g513: Не объясняя is a negative adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'without explaining'; it sets the circumstance for the main clause. Мне is the dative 'to me', and ничего is the negated pronoun 'nothing' (object of объясняя).
segments.p2-7-g514: Как будто introduces a parenthetical comparative clause 'as if'; я is the subject of that subordinate clause. Будто signals that what follows is hypothetical or imagined.
segments.p2-7-g515: Как какое-нибудь высшее существо is a simile inside the parenthesis: как 'like', какое-нибудь is an indefinite pronoun 'some/any' modifying существо 'being/creature', and высшее is the neuter singular adjective 'higher/loftier' agreeing with существо — together 'like some higher being'.
segments.p2-7-g516: Должен был знать is a modal past construction: должен (modal 'ought to / supposed to') + был (past auxiliary) + infinitive знать 'to know' → 'was supposed to know'. Все is the object 'everything', and без объяснений is a prepositional phrase 'without explanations' (без + genitive).
segments.p2-7-g517: This returns to the main clause: она (she) is the subject; протянула is the feminine past tense of протянуть meaning 'handed out/held out/extended' (completed action); мне is the dative 'to me'; бумажку is the accusative singular diminutive 'a slip/piece of paper'.
segments.p2-7-g518: Все лицо ее is 'her whole face' (весь + noun with the possessor ее following the noun, a common literary order). Так и просияло: так и adds emphasis to the verb просияло (past tense neuter agreeing with лицо) meaning 'lit up' or 'shone'. В это мгновение is a temporal phrase 'at that moment'. Самым наивным is the superlative instrumental ('with the most naive') and anticipates the noun торжеством in the same case.
segments.p2-7-g519: Почти modifies детским ('almost childlike') and торжеством is the instrumental singular of торжество meaning 'triumph/celebration'; together they complete the instrumental phrase 'with the most naive, almost childlike triumph' describing the manner in which the face lit up.
segments.p2-7-g470: Я is the 1st-person singular subject pronoun. встал is the past‑tense, perfective form of встать in the masculine singular, meaning 'got up' or 'stood up' and agrees with a male speaker; word order Subject‑Verb is straightforward here.
segments.p2-7-g471: встала is the past feminine singular of встать, showing that 'she' also rose; the following и она repeats the subject for emphasis (verb before pronoun is stylistic). The second и links to the next clause; вдруг = 'suddenly'. вся here is an intensifier agreeing in gender with the woman and means 'all (over)'; закраснелась is the past feminine of закраснеться (perfective, reflexive) meaning 'blushed/reddened.'
segments.p2-7-g472: вздрогнула is a single past‑tense feminine verb (вздрогнуть), meaning 'gave a start' or 'shuddered'; standing alone as a one‑word clause it expresses a quick involuntary reaction.
segments.p2-7-g473: This is a compound sequence of actions. схватила is past feminine of схватить 'grabbed'; лежавший is the past active participle modifying платок (masculine nominative), 'lying (on the chair)'; на стуле uses на + locative to mark location. платок is the direct object. и introduces the next verb; набросила is past feminine of набросить 'threw on, draped'; себе is the dative reflexive meaning 'to/for herself' (benefactive), and на плечи is the direction 'onto the shoulders.' до самого подбородка is a prepositional phrase 'up to the very chin,' with самого as an intensifier.
segments.p2-7-g474: Сделав is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) from сделать meaning 'having done'; it indicates a completed action that precedes the main verb. это is the pronoun 'this/it' and is the object of the gerund.
segments.p2-7-g475: она is the subject 'she'; опять means 'again.' как-то is an adverbial combination meaning 'somehow' or 'in some way' (как + particle то functioning as an indefinite modifier). болезненно is an adverb modifying улыбнулась meaning 'painfully' or 'in a sickly way'; улыбнулась is past feminine of улыбнуться 'smiled.'
segments.p2-7-g476: покраснела is past feminine of покраснеть 'blushed/turned red' and is coordinated with the following verb by и. странно is an adverb 'strangely/oddly.' поглядела is past feminine of поглядеть 'glanced/looked' and на меня marks the object 'at me' with the preposition на + accusative.
segments.p2-7-g477: Мне было больно is an impersonal construction: мне = 'to me', было = past tense of 'there was/it was', and больно is an adverb meaning 'painful' or 'it hurt'. Together they mean 'I was hurt / it hurt me.'
segments.p2-7-g478: я is the subject 'I'; спешил is the past imperfective masculine form of спешить 'was hurrying' (shows haste or repeated/ongoing aspect in the past); уйти is the perfective infinitive 'to leave,' expressing the intended completed action.
segments.p2-7-g479: стушеваться is an infinitive reflexive verb meaning 'to become embarrassed / to withdraw/slink away'; standing alone here it states the speaker's aim or manner of leaving.
segments.p2-7-g480: Подождите is the imperative plural (or polite singular) of подождать meaning 'wait (a moment)' or 'please wait'; punctuation shows direct speech.
segments.p2-7-g481: сказала is past feminine of сказать 'she said'; она repeats the subject; вдруг 'suddenly' indicates the manner/timing of speech and follows the verb for emphasis.
segments.p2-7-g482: уже = 'already'; в сенях is locative plural (old/colloquial form сеня/сенях) meaning 'in the entry‑hall'; у самых дверей is a close‑proximity phrase ('by the very doors'), with самых as an intensifier 'very/right.'
segments.p2-7-g483: останавливая is a present gerund (деепричастие) from останавливать meaning 'stopping/detaining' and expresses a simultaneous action; меня is the object 'me'; рукою is an archaic instrumental form of рука ('with her hand'); за шинель indicates the point she grabbed (lit. 'by/at the greatcoat').
segments.p2-7-g484: поставила is past feminine of поставить 'placed/set (down)'; впопыхах is an adverb 'in a rush/hastily'; свечу is the accusative object 'a candle.' и links to убежала, past feminine of убежать 'ran off/ran away.'
segments.p2-7-g485: видно is a parenthetical/inferential adverb meaning 'apparently' or 'it seems', marking the narrator's conjecture about her motive.
segments.p2-7-g486: вспомнила is past feminine of вспомнить 'remembered'; про что‑то is 'about something' (про + indefinite что‑то), with что functioning as 'what/what‑ever' in the indefinite; или introduces the alternative; хотела is past imperfective of хотеть 'wanted/intended'; мне is dative 'for me/to me'; принести показать is an infinitive sequence 'to bring (and) to show', where the object 'something' is implied from про что‑то.
segments.p2-7-g520: Я is the 1st-person singular subject 'I'. развернул is the masculine past tense form of the perfective verb развернуть ('to unfold/open'); together they form a short past-clause 'I unfolded (it)'. The object ('it') is omitted because it is understood from the context (the letter mentioned before).
segments.p2-7-g521: Это было письмо is a copular identification 'It was a letter' (Это here functions as the dummy/identifying pronoun). к ней 'to her' uses the preposition к + pronoun (dative-like usage after к). от какого-то медицинского студента uses the indefinite combination какого + то (какого-то) to mean 'some' modifying медицинского студента; или в этом роде is a fixed phrase 'or of that sort'. Word order is neutral: identification → indirect object → agent phrase → parenthetical 'or of that sort'.
segments.p2-7-g522: очень (very) modifies the adjective высокопарное; высокопарное is a neuter adjective agreeing with письмо and means 'high‑flown' or 'pompous'. The comma indicates a series of adjectival descriptions of the letter.
segments.p2-7-g523: цветистое is another neuter adjective agreeing with письмо, meaning 'flowery' (ornate language). It continues the descriptive series and is set off by commas.
segments.p2-7-g524: но introduces a contrast ('but'). чрезвычайно is an adverb intensifier ('extremely') modifying почтительное. почтительное объяснение literally 'a respectful explanation'; the phrase объяснение в любви is idiomatic for 'a declaration of love' ('в любви' uses the noun любовь in the genitive after the preposition to form this set phrase). The whole segment contrasts ornate style with sincere respectfulness.
segments.p2-7-g525: Не припомню теперь выражений is a negated memory statement: припомню is the perfective form (literally future) but in this idiomatic context functions as 'I cannot recall' or 'I don't remember'. теперь 'now' locates the inability to recall in the present. выражений is the plural noun (genitive) 'expressions/phrases' — here meaning the precise wording.
segments.p2-7-g526: но ('but') introduces the contrast. помню is the imperfective present 'I remember' (1st person), and очень хорошо ('very well') intensifies it: 'but I remember very well'. This balances the previous negation about exact words with a positive about the impression.
segments.p2-7-g527: что introduces the content clause 'that...'. сквозь ('through/despite') governs высокий слог: высокий is 'lofty/elevated' and слог here means 'style' or 'tone'. проглядывало is the neuter past imperfective (agreeing with the neuter subject that follows) meaning 'peeped through' or 'could be glimpsed'. истинное чувство is the nominative subject 'a true feeling' that 'showed through' the lofty style.
segments.p2-7-g528: которого is a relative pronoun referring back to чувство; here it appears in the genitive form (которого) because the verblike expression that follows is negated and the construction treats the object as genitive. не + подделаешь is the 2nd-person singular form of the perfective verb подделать used generically ('you can't fake'). The clause means 'which you can't fake.'
segments.p2-7-g529: Когда introduces a temporal clause 'when'. я is the subject 'I'. дочитал is the perfective past masculine singular of дочитать 'to finish reading', so this clause means 'when I had finished reading' (completed action).
segments.p2-7-g530: то is a narrative particle meaning roughly 'then' and adds a sequential link; встретил is the past masculine singular 'I encountered/met' (the verb is in active form but the natural English translation uses a passive-like 'was met by'). горячий is an adjective 'hot/ardent' placed before the noun it modifies; word order here is adjective(s) + noun coming next.
segments.p2-7-g531: любопытный and детски-нетерпеливый are adjectives modifying взгляд (the latter is a hyphenated compound where детски 'childishly/childlike' qualifies нетерпеливый 'impatient/eager'). взгляд is the noun 'gaze'. ее is the possessive 'her' (postposed here as is common in Russian), and на себе literally 'on oneself' functions here reflexively to mean 'directed at me' — 'her gaze on me'. Together: 'a hot, curious, childishly impatient gaze of hers directed at me.'
segments.p2-7-g532: Она is the nominative feminine pronoun 'she' (subject). приковалась is a past-tense, reflexive verb (приковаться) meaning 'fixed (one's gaze)'; it is followed by the instrumental phrase глазами to mark the means ('with her eyes' / 'by her gaze'). к + dative (к моему лицу) indicates direction toward the face. и links to the next predicate; в нетерпении is a prepositional/adverbial phrase ('in impatience' = 'impatiently') modifying ждала, which is the past feminine form 'waited/was waiting' (imperfective, indicating an ongoing or habitual waiting). The dash introduces the direct question Что я скажу? where что is 'what' and скажу is the first-person singular future/Perfective 'I will say' used here as a rhetorical question.
segments.p2-7-g533: В нескольких словах is a prepositional phrase (в + prepositional plural) meaning 'in a few words' or 'briefly'; словах is the prepositional plural of слово and here expresses manner ('in a few words').
segments.p2-7-g534: наскоро is an adverb meaning 'quickly' or 'hastily' and modifies the main verb объяснила that follows.
segments.p2-7-g535: но как-то радостно и как будто гордясь contains coordinating and modifying particles: но = 'but'; как-то = 'somehow' or 'in a way' (как + particle то giving an indistinct/qualifying sense); радостно = adverb 'joyfully'; и = 'and'; как будто = 'as if'; гордясь is a gerund (деепричастие) from гордиться meaning 'being proud' or 'with an air of pride', indicating a concurrent manner while she explained.
segments.p2-7-g536: она объяснила мне is the main clause: она (she) is the subject; объяснила is past tense, perfective, meaning 'explained' (a completed act); мне is the dative 'to me' marking the indirect object.
segments.p2-7-g537: что была где-то на танцевальном вечере begins the content clause introduced by что ('that'); была is past tense 'was' (she had been); где-то ('where' + particle то) is 'somewhere'; на танцевальном вечере = 'at a dancing evening' i.e. at a dance/ball (на + prepositional).
segments.p2-7-g538: в семейном доме is a locative phrase (в + prepositional) meaning 'in/at a family home' or 'in a private/home of a family' (семейном = family/domestic, доме = house/home).
segments.p2-7-g539: у одних 'очень, starts a reported quotation: у одних literally 'at some people's / at the place of certain people' (у + genitive одних meaning 'certain ones' or 'some people'); very (очень) begins the quoted description.
segments.p2-7-g540: очень хороших людей continues the quoted description: очень 'very' intensifies; хороших is genitive plural adjective 'good/kind' modifying людей (genitive plural of человек) 'people'.
segments.p2-7-g541: семейных людей и где ничего еще не знают continues: семейных людей repeats 'family people' or 'family-oriented people'; и links to the relative clause где ... не знают ('where they know nothing yet') — где introduces the relative/location clause and ничегo ... не знают is the negative present 'they don't know anything yet' (ничего = 'nothing', еще = 'yet', не = negation, знают = 'know').
segments.p2-7-g542: совсем ничего is an intensifying negative phrase: совсем = 'completely/at all' and ничего = 'nothing', giving the sense 'absolutely nothing.'
segments.p2-7-g543: - потому что она и здесь-то еще только внове и только так gives the reason: потому что = 'because'; она = 'she'; и here functions as 'even/also' or simply connective; здесь-то uses the particle то for emphasis ('here, of all places'); еще только внове is 'only just/newly' (внове is an older/rarer adverb meaning 'anew/recently'); and только так = 'only in that way' or 'only like that.'
segments.p2-7-g546: а вовсе еще не решилась остаться и непременно уйдет is a compound clause: а = 'but' (contrastive); вовсе еще не (вовсе + еще + не) intensifies the negation ('still not at all'); решилась is past reflexive/perfective 'decided' (negated here = 'hasn't decided'); остаться is the infinitive 'to stay'; и links to the following future clause непременно уйдет where непременно = 'certainly/without fail' and уйдет is future indicative (3rd sing.) 'will leave'. Note the contrast between the negated past (hasn't decided) and the simple future (will leave).
segments.p2-7-g547: как только долг заплатит is a temporal subordinate clause: как только = 'as soon as' (conjunction), долг = 'the debt' (here monetary obligation), and заплатит is the perfective future (3rd singular) 'he/she/it pays' or 'pays off', marking the condition that will trigger her leaving.
segments.p2-7-g550: "Ну и там был этот студент is an opening exclamation: Ну = 'well' (colloquial interjection); и adds an emphatic/adversative sense ('and/too'); там = 'there'; был = past 'was'; этот студент = 'this/that student' (demonstrative + noun) identifying the person mentioned.
segments.p2-7-g551: весь вечер танцевал is a simple time-expression + verb: весь вечер = 'the whole evening' (весь = whole/all, вечер = evening/time), and танцевал is the past tense 'danced' (he danced the whole evening).
segments.p2-7-g552: говорил с ней is 'he spoke/talked with her': говорил is past tense of говорить and here means 'talked/conversed', с is the preposition 'with', ней is the prepositional/dative form of 'она' used after prepositions ('her').
segments.p2-7-g553: и оказалось is a linking clause meaning 'and it turned out' or 'and it happened that' — оказалось is impersonal/past of оказаться 'to turn out/to prove to be', introducing the following revelation.
segments.p2-7-g554: что он еще в Риге introduces the content: что = 'that'; он = 'he'; еще = 'still'; в Риге = 'in Riga' (locative), i.e. 'that he is still in Riga.'
segments.p2-7-g555: еще ребенком был с ней знаком means 'he had known her as a child': еще ребенком is an adverbial phrase ('when still a child/as a child'); был is past 'was'; с ней знаком is a predicative construction where знаком (short form adjective) means 'acquainted/familiar with' and с ней = 'with her'.
segments.p2-7-g556: вместе играли is 'they played together': вместе = 'together' (adverb), играли = past plural 'played', indicating shared activity in the past.
segments.p2-7-g557: только уж очень давно qualifies the previous clause: только = 'only/just', уж = an intensifier often 'already' or 'indeed' here adding emphasis, очень = 'very', давно = 'long ago' — together meaning 'only that it was a very long time ago.'
segments.p2-7-g558: - и родителей ее знает is parenthetical: и = 'and' (adding information), родителей ее = 'her parents' (родителей genitive plural + ее 'her'), and знает = 'knows' (present tense), meaning he is acquainted with her parents.
segments.p2-7-g559: но что об этом он ничего-ничего-ничего не знает и не подозревает! is the exclamatory final clause: но что = 'but that'; об этом = 'about this' (об + prepositional это); он = 'he'; ничего-ничего-ничего ... не знает is emphatic repetition meaning 'knows absolutely nothing'; и не подозревает = 'and does not suspect' (подозревает is present tense 'suspect'/'have a notion'). The triple repetition is rhetorical emphasis.
segments.p2-7-g571: Бедненькая is a diminutive adjective used as an exclamation/epithet meaning 'poor little thing' or 'poor woman' (nominative singular feminine). Here it stands alone as a sympathetic remark before the main clause.
